title: Tithymalus bahamensis

taxonomy: Tithymalus bahamensis is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tithymalus, which is now considered a synonym for the genus Euphorbia. Therefore, Tithymalus bahamensis is also classified as Euphorbia bahamensis. The Euphorbiaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. This classification system is widely accepted in the field of botany.
uses: Tithymalus bahamensis, also known as the Bahamian Spurge, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its sap is said to be used to treat skin conditions, including eczema and acne. The plant's latex is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling.
